evaluations.signal_reason: QQQ holding above $620 support with NVIDIA breaking to new highs driving tech rally. VIX compressed to 19.03 confirms risk-on sentiment.
evaluations.summary: <cite index="6-1,9-5">QQQ trading at $661.24, down -0.40% today but holding well above the $620 invalidation level.</cite> <cite index="13-2,13-24">NVIDIA stock rose 5% on April 24 pushing market cap to $5.09 trillion, approaching all-time highs at $209 per share.</cite> <cite index="25-3,22-3">VIX compressed from previous highs to 19.03, supporting the thesis of renewed risk appetite.</cite>
evaluations.updated_bear_case: <cite index="13-9,13-17">NVIDIA dramatically underperformed other semiconductors YTD at only 7% vs Marvell 95%, as investors diversify away from single dominant names into broader AI infrastructure plays.</cite>

evaluations.signal_reason: NVDA hasn't reported April 23 earnings yet - thesis catalyst premature. Heavy AI spending concerns emerging across tech ahead of Big Tech earnings.
evaluations.summary: QQQ at $654 remains well above $620 invalidation but the thesis is built on a false catalyst. NVDA earnings aren't until May 20th, not April 23rd as stated. Tech sector faces growing scrutiny over massive AI capex with major hyperscalers reporting Wednesday.
evaluations.updated_bear_case: AI 'ROI reckoning' narrative gaining traction as analysts question whether $650B+ AI spending translates to revenue. Free cash flow compression expected across Big Tech as infrastructure spending outpaces returns.

evaluations.signal_reason: Tech momentum intact despite daily decline. NVDA beat earnings driving sector growth, QQQ at strong levels above original thesis.
evaluations.summary: QQQ closed at $657.55, down -1.03% today but significantly above thesis entry of $648. NVIDIA's Q4 2026 blowout earnings with $68.1B revenue (+73% YoY) and $62.3B data center revenue validates AI thesis. Next NVDA earnings May 20th creates positive catalyst runway.
evaluations.updated_bear_case: Rising competition narrative emerging with Alphabet's TPU v8 announcement and stretched valuations at 25x forward P/E despite strong fundamentals.

evaluations.signal_reason: NVDA delivered exceptional Q1 results with $68B revenue but guidance shows slight deceleration. QQQ holding steady near highs despite rising VIX.
evaluations.summary: QQQ at $662.23, holding well above $620 invalidation level. NVDA earnings catalyst delivered with record $68.1B Q4 revenue (+73% YoY) but Q1 guidance of $78B shows modest deceleration from recent quarters. VIX rising to 18.8 from prior 17 levels on tech earnings uncertainty and Iran tensions, but remains well below March peak of 31.
evaluations.updated_bear_case: VIX climbing back toward 20 amid concentrated earnings risk from Mag 7 and geopolitical tensions pushing oil above $110. Some concerns emerging about AI spending sustainability with OpenAI missing user/revenue targets.
evaluations.key_level_to_watch: $650 support - former ATH now key technical level

evaluations.signal_reason: QQQ broke above ATH at $675.76, VIX compressed to 17.1 (-9.1% today), NVDA May 20 earnings catalyst intact with 77-80% revenue growth expected.
evaluations.summary: <cite index="7-1,7-2">QQQ hit new ATH at $675.76 today trading at $674.78 vs yesterday's close of $667.74</cite>. <cite index="24-1">VIX collapsed -9.1% to 17.10</cite>, confirming risk-on sentiment. <cite index="11-1,17-2">NVDA earnings confirmed for May 20 with guidance for 77% revenue growth</cite>.
evaluations.updated_bear_case: <cite index="14-4,18-7">Competition concerns from Amazon and Google heating up, potentially weighing on NVDA margins</cite>. <cite index="18-10">High valuations may limit upside even with strong earnings</cite>.
evaluations.key_level_to_watch: $670 support - former resistance now key support
